cot_prefix: Okay, let's figure this out. So the question is about Jane's birthday and figuring out the date one week from her 16th birthday. Let me break it down step by step. First, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2000. Now, February usually has 28 days, but in a leap year, it has 29. Since 2000 is a leap year (because years divisible by 100 are only leap years if they're also divisible by 400, and 2000 is divisible by 400), the last day of February 2000 would be February 29, 2000. So Jane's birthday is on 02/29/2000. Today is her 16th birthday. But wait, 2000 + 16 is 2016. So her 16th birthday would be in 2016. However, since she was born on February 29, which only occurs every four years, on non-leap years, her birthday would be celebrated on either February 28 or March 1. The question says "today is her 16-year-old birthday," so we need to determine if 2016 is a leap year. Let's check: 2016 divided by 4 is 504, no remainder, so it is a leap year. Therefore, her 16th birthday is on February 29, 2016. Now, the question asks for the date one week from today (her birthday). If today is February 29, 2016, adding one week (7 days) would take us to March 7, 2016. Wait, let me count: - February 29 + 1 day is March 1 - Then adding 6 more days: March 1 + 6 days = March 7. So one week from February 29, 2016, would be March 7, 2016. Let's check the options. Option A is 03/07/2016. But wait, let me make sure there's no mistake here. But another thought: Some people might think that since February 29 doesn't exist in non-leap years, maybe the birthday is celebrated on February 28, 2016. However, since 2016 is a leap year, her actual birthday does occur on February 29. So adding seven days would take us to March 7. Let me check each day: February 29 (Mon?), but I don't need the day of the week. Just the date. So adding 7 days to 02/29/2016: Day 1: 03/01/2016 Then adding 6 more days would be 03/07/2016. So
